Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Expected Sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Observed Sequence in Case 1002 1 Create Purchase Requisition Feb 1 0830 2 Issue Purchase Order Feb 1 0840 skips approval 3 Vendor Invoice Received Feb 3 0700 before approval or receipt 4 Release Payment Feb 3 0715 before approval or receipt 5 Approve Purchase Requisition Feb 3 0800 approval occurs after payment and invoice 6 Receive Goods Feb 4 0900 after invoice and payment 7 Match Invoice to PO Feb 5 0800 after payment and receipt 8 Close Case Feb 6 1600

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The approval of the purchase requisition occurs after the invoice has already been received and after a payment has been released In standard P2P practices approval is required before any commitment issuing a PO or incurring liability Releasing payment before approval creates a risk of unauthorized spending and potential fraud

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payment is released before the invoice is matched to the PO which contradicts the three way match PO receipt invoice that is a cornerstone of P2P controls to prevent overpayment or duplicate payments

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The vendor invoice is received before the goods are received invoice on Feb 3 goods on Feb 4 which is highly unusual unless its a pre billed service or an error

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The fact that the approval occurs after payment implies either A system misconfiguration workflow not enforced Manual override bypassing controls Or a failure in process ownership

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The sequence suggests a breakdown in process discipline The organization may be operating in a reactive or ad hoc manner possibly due to pressure poor system enforcement or lack of training

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This increases the risk of Overpayment Duplicate invoices Unauthorized purchases Audit failures

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1002 is anomalous because it reverses the logical and operational sequence of critical P2P controls Specifically Approval occurs too late after payment Invoice is received before goods risk of billing for undelivered goods Payment occurs before matching violating the three way match principle

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Other cases 1001 1003 1004 follow a more logical sequence with approvals occurring before invoice receipt and payment and goods received before or concurrent with invoice receipt Thus Case 1002 stands out as the primary anomaly
